title: Poitiers
content: Poitiers (,,,; Poitevin: "Poetàe") is a city on the Clain river in west-central France. It is a commune and the capital of the Vienne department and the historical centre of Poitou. In 2017 it had a population of 88,291. Its agglomeration has 130,853 inhabitants in 2016 and is the center of an urban area of 261,795 inhabitants. With more than 29,000 students, Poitiers has been a major university city since the creation of its University in 1431, having hosted René Descartes, Joachim du Bellay and François Rabelais, among others.
